name: GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop
name_original: Pantano de Riba-roja – circular por Flix y el mirador de la Ribera d'Ebre
description: This moderate 26.1-mile mountain biking route circles Pantano de Riba-roja, passing through Flix and offering panoramic Ebro River views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Flix are characterized by the Ebro River valley, which offers varied terrain for cycling. The region features extensive trail networks, including routes near the Pantano de Riba-roja and Pantano de la Palma de Ebro. Mountainous areas like the Montsant Mountains and Serra de Cardó provide challenging backdrops, while the Sebes Natural Reserve offers routes through local flora and fauna. The landscape combines riverine paths with more rugged, elevated sections. Best mountain bike trails around Flix The most popular mountain bike trail is GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop, a 26.1 miles (42.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the areas around Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pantano de la Palma de Ebro – circular por la cueva de Hospital Santa Llúcia, a moderate 18.6 miles (30.0 km) path. This circular route offers views around the Pantano de la Palma de Ebro. Local mountain bikers also love the Riba-roja d'Ebre – Sebes Nature Reserve Boardwalk loop from Flix, a 12.9 miles (20.8 km) trail leading through the Sebes Natural Reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Flix is defined by the Ebro River, the Montsant Mountains, and the Sebes Natural Reserve. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Flix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Flix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Flix?
A: Mountain bike trails around Flix offer a varied landscape, characterized by the Ebro River valley. You'll find a mix of riverine paths and more rugged, elevated sections. The region includes routes near the Pantano de Riba-roja and Pantano de la Palma de Ebro, with mountainous backdrops provided by areas like the Montsant Mountains and Serra de Cardó. Some trails are specifically adapted for enduro riding, featuring singletrack and diverse surfaces.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Flix area?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails available around Flix, catering to various skill levels. This includes 8 easy routes, 42 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes, ensuring options for every type of rider.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Flix?
A: Yes, Flix offers several easy mountain bike trails. One excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38243491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja d'Ebre – Sebes Nature Reserve Boardwalk loop from Flix&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.9 miles (20.8 km) long and leads through the scenic Sebes Natural Reserve. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38247951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja d'Ebre – Sebes Nature Reserve Boardwalk loop from Riba-roja d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21.1 km long.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Flix?
A: The mountain bike trails in Flix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. Over 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Flix's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Flix?
A: Yes, the region features several circular routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955890171/pantano-de-la-palma-de-ebro-circular-por-la-cueva-de-hospital-santa-llucia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de la Palma de Ebro – circular por la cueva de Hospital Santa Llúcia&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.6 miles (30.0 km) long and offers views around the Pantano de la Palma de Ebro. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944315935/gr-99-flix-and-riba-roja-d-ebre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.1 miles (42.0 km) moderate trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Flix?
A: The area around Flix is rich in natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You can explore the Sebes Natural Reserve, ride along the Ebro River, or encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/843527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadolles Fondes Deep Pools&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts several caves, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5086443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3224462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clot del Cirer&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or viewpoints accessible from mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, you can combine your ride with visits to historical sites or scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/305718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escaladei Charterhouse&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/843525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Bartomeu de Fraguerau&lt;/a&gt; are notable historical attractions. For panoramic views, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4584288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Priorat Balcony&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Flix?
A: Flix offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. While the region is suitable for outdoor activities, it's advisable to check local weather forecasts, especially during summer when temperatures can be high, or in winter for cooler conditions.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Flix?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Flix area. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural reserves like Sebes, and to clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for all users and to protect local wildlife.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Flix and the broader Ribera d'Ebre region offer difficult trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31512086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja d'Ebre – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Riba-roja d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.0 km route with significant elevation gain. The Serra de Cardó and Els Ports Natural Park also provide suitable landscapes for intense mountain biking days and enduro descents.

Q: Is there public transport access to mountain bike trails in Flix?
A: Information on specific public transport links directly to trailheads is limited. However, Flix is a municipality with some public transport connections. For accessing trails, it's often most convenient to start from the town center or use personal transport to reach specific starting points. The Via Verde del Baix Ebre, a converted railway line, offers a car-free route that can be accessed at various points along its 44 km stretch.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Ebro River?
A: Yes, the Ebro River is a central feature of the landscape around Flix, and many mountain bike routes incorporate its valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944315935/gr-99-flix-and-riba-roja-d-ebre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores areas around Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre along the river. The Sebes Natural Reserve, which features a boardwalk, is also situated along the Ebro, offering pleasant riverine cycling experiences.
tips: Flix Castle is a walled fortification located on a hill near the center of Flix. Its geographical location made the castle one of the most important in the region.

The Sebes natural area is located on the left side of the Ebro river, near the municipality of Flix. A river space that preserves a good sample of vegetation that includes an area of wetlands, some river islands and a protected river area of about sixty meters.

The Riba-roja reservoir is located between Aragon and Catalonia. The reservoir has a hydroelectric power plant, which is why its main use is power generation, although it is also used for irrigation.
and water supply to the towns in the area.

faqs.question: How long does this mountain biking route typically take?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route, covering approximately 42 km with an elevation gain of about 747 meters, typically takes around 3 hours and 51 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Pantano de Riba-roja circular route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it features varied terrain including old carriage roads and dirt tracks, some sections involve an abrupt descent and remote paths, suggesting a balanced experience that can be both restful and adventurous. Riders should be prepared for a mix of surfaces.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied terrain, including old carriage roads and abandoned stock routes. You'll traverse a mix of paths and dirt tracks, some of which may be remote. Expect sections with an abrupt descent down the slopes of Escambriu to the Ebro riverbank, followed by a towpath along the river. The route also winds through almond groves and includes secondary tracks, as well as descents along the shady slope of Escambrons.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable sights and panoramic viewpoints. Highlights include the historic Flix Castle, the scenic Viewpoint of Sebes Nature Reserve, and the expansive Riba-roja Reservoir. The route also offers breathtaking vistas from viewpoints like the 'mirador de Pauls y Fatarella' and the 'mirador del embalse de Riba-roja', showcasing the winding Ebro River and lush glades.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations are not detailed, the route is circular and passes through the villages of Riba-roja d'Ebre and Flix. It is generally advisable to look for public parking within these villages, which serve as convenient starting and ending points for the loop.

faqs.question: Is the Pantano de Riba-roja – circular por Flix y el mirador de la Ribera d'Ebre route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, especially if kept on a leash. Given the natural areas and potential for wildlife, it's recommended to keep your dog under control. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, with vibrant natural colors. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter rides are also possible, but check weather conditions for rain or colder temperatures.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride this mountain biking trail. It is generally open to the public for recreational use.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with official trails such as E37, TransCatalunya. De Ponent, and Vall de Sant Francesc, among others. This indicates a well-established network for outdoor activities in the area.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or natural features to look out for along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The Ebro River itself is a significant historical feature, having served as a crucial 'bi-directional highway' for centuries. You'll also pass through the Natural Interest Area of Sebes, which is excellent for bird watching, and experience the cultural charm of villages like Riba-roja d'Ebre and Flix, nestled within a narrow meander of the river.
